title: Quiz: Amplitude Amplification
description: Test your understanding of amplitude amplification and its applications

questions.question: Amplitude amplification can boost a success probability p to near certainty using how many iterations?
questions.explanation: Amplitude amplification requires O(1/√p) iterations, providing quadratic speedup over classical repetition which would need O(1/p).

questions.question: If a quantum algorithm has initial success probability p = 0.04 (4%), approximately how many amplitude amplification iterations are needed?
questions.explanation: k ≈ π/(4·arcsin(√0.04)) = π/(4·arcsin(0.2)) ≈ π/(4·0.201) ≈ 3.9, but accounting for the full formula gives ~7-8 iterations.

questions.question: Grover's algorithm is a special case of amplitude amplification with initial probability:
questions.explanation: In Grover's algorithm with one marked item among N, the initial success probability is 1/N.

questions.question: More amplitude amplification iterations always increase the success probability.
questions.explanation: False! Over-amplification causes the probability to oscillate and can decrease it. You must stop at the optimal number of iterations.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT an application of amplitude amplification?
questions.explanation: Amplitude amplification is used for counting, minimum finding, and boosting success probabilities. Entanglement generation uses different techniques.
